data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>`.** Both compiled and ran without issues on .NET 8.

- `Span<nint> nints = stackalloc nint[4];` ✅
- `Span<nuint> nuints = stackalloc nuint[3];` ✅

The values (including `nint.MaxValue` / `nuint.MaxValue`) printed correctly, confirming they're 8 bytes (64-bit) on this platform. This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types backed by `System.IntPtr`/`System.UIntPtr`.
